hero_subtitle: SFTY helps digital asset businesses enforce retention policies with role-based access, audit logs, and evidence integrity to support institutional governance and bank due diligence.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk

problem_title: Compliance data retention lacks clear governance
problem_description: Regulated digital asset businesses struggle to enforce consistent data retention rules across provider outputs and case records. This creates gaps in audit trails, complicates bank due diligence and weakens operational control over sensitive compliance information.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes retention policies, preserves audit logs and provider outputs, and applies role-based access controls to maintain a secure, traceable and consistent compliance data governance framework.
workflow_title: From data retention policy to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures compliance data retention by enforcing rules that preserve audit logs, control access permissions, and maintain evidence integrity for institutional review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for compliance data retention?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures compliance data retention by preserving case records, decisions, and evidence with role-based access and configurable retention policies suited for regulated digital asset firms.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage compliance case evidence?
faq_2_answer: SFTY collects, links and preserves provider outputs, workflows, and reviewer actions in cases to create a complete evidence package for compliance reviews and operational traceability.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY support audit logs and traceability?
faq_3_answer: SFTY maintains detailed audit logs capturing user actions, decision history, rule changes and evidence updates to support review readiness and internal or external inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing onboarding, KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening tools, connecting their outputs into one connected compliance operating layer without disrupting current systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace compliance data providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, decision logs and audit-ready evidence for regulated enterprises managing compliance data retention.
final_title: Structure compliance data retention with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY supports role-based access, audit logs, evidence integrity and data governance for institutional digital asset compliance.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
